library_preparation_preflight.summary: No extra setup is required: the artifact is the Maven SCM Git executable provider, its runtime dependencies are resolved transitively, and meaningful coverage is reachable through command-line factory, parser/consumer, repository URL, and provider behavior tests using local temporary files without additional Maven dependencies, Docker services, or required system properties.
risks: Tests that execute real Git operations instead of only constructing/parsing commands will rely on a `git` executable on `PATH` and may need local temporary-repository configuration such as `user.name` and `user.email`; that is test-code setup, not driver-level deterministic setup.

The reported javac failure is caused by Maven SCM 2.2.1 API changes such as `GitTagCommand.createCommandLine` now taking `CommandParameters.SignOption` and `createPushCommandLine` being instance-based, not by missing setup.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8354 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.scm:maven-scm-provider-gitexe:2.2.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015
